engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz Vito Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz Vito across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced 2010 for W639/W447 Vito, delivering 95-136hp with common-rail injection and Euro 5 compliance.
compatibleVariants: 110 CDI, 110 CDI Panel Van, 110 CDI Crew Van, 113 CDI, 113 CDI Panel Van, 113 CDI Crew Van, 113 CDI Traveliner
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch/tensioner failure (cold-start rattle, 80k-120k miles)
Injector seal blow-by causing carbon buildup (100k+ miles)
EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss (90k+ miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651.940 Mercedes Benz Vito engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel

compatibleVariants: 116 CDI, 116 CDI Panel Van, 116 CDI Crew Van, 116 CDI Traveliner, 119 CDI, 119 CDI Panel Van, 119 CDI Traveliner
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner seal leak (REC-005099 recall, 70k-100k miles)
Turbocharger bearing wear under heavy load (110k+ miles)
DPF bracket fatigue causing exhaust rattle (90k+ miles)

commonFailures: Timing chain elongation causing rattling noise (90k-130k miles)

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz Vito Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes Benz Vito is a robust commercial workhorse, but three engine families consistently dominate UK replacement demand: the OM651 2.1L diesel, the older OM646 2.2L diesel, and the modern OM654 2.0L unit. Timing system wear, EGR thermal stress, and high-pressure fuel faults are the primary drivers for engine swaps on this chassis. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, allowing you to choose between targeted repair or full replacement with confidence.

problems.h4: OM651 Timing Chain Failure - Why Mercedes Benz Vito Engines Rattle on Cold Start
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Vito 113 CDI, 116 CDI, 119 CDI (W639 late facelift & W447, 2010-present, OM651.940 & OM651.955)
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain runs in an oil-starved zone when the tensioner seal leaks, causing rapid chain stretch and plastic guide wear. If ignored, the chain jumps teeth, leading to catastrophic valve and piston interference.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine-out access, full chain kit, updated tensioner and guides fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; extends life but does not fix already stretched guides

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner seals, bench-tested, ECU coded
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; addresses all internal wear with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty

problems.recommendation: We recommend replacing the chain assembly only if cold-start rattle is recent and cylinder compression remains stable across all banks. On a Mercedes Benz Vito with confirmed chain jump, metallic knocking, or diagnostic timing deviation codes, a rebuilt OM651 is the only economically sound choice given the extensive labour required for engine-out diagnostics and the high risk of repeat failure with stock components.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM651 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz Vito

problems.h4: OM646 Timing Chain Elongation - Mercedes Benz Vito Pre-2010 Diesel Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Vito 111 CDI, 115 CDI (W639, 2003-2014, OM646.980 & OM646.982)
problems.rootCause: The single-row timing chain gradually elongates under sustained high-load commercial use, overwhelming the hydraulic tensioner and causing misalignment that risks catastrophic interference failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front-access chain replacement with upgraded tensioner and hardened guide rails
repairOptions.longevity: Restores timing accuracy; only recommended before interference or cam damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with reinforced timing components, supply & fit, dyno tested
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves elongation and secondary bottom-end wear; best long-term value for older chassis

problems.recommendation: For a Mercedes Benz Vito W639 approaching high mileage, preventative chain replacement often exceeds the vehicle’s residual commercial value. We advise a full rebuilt OM646 swap once rattle is constant at idle or if fault logs show timing deviation, as it eliminates recurring tensioner faults and restores full payload reliability without hidden ancillary costs.
problems.cta: Compare OM646 Mercedes Benz Vito replacement costs

problems.group: OM651 Injector Seal & EGR Cooler Failure
problems.h4: OM651 Injector Seal & EGR Cooler Failure - Mercedes Benz Vito Carbon & Coolant Loss
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Vito 110-119 CDI (W447 & late W639, 2010-present, OM651 family)
problems.rootCause: Degrading injector copper seals allow combustion gases into the crankcase, carbonising pistons and oil rings. Simultaneously, the EGR cooler develops thermal micro-cracks, mixing coolant with exhaust gases and reducing combustion efficiency.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Injector Seals & EGR Cooler Swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Seal replacement, upgraded cooler installation, engine oil flush to remove sludge
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before ring damage or severe overheating; stops progression

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply & fit, updated seals, tested EGR/coolant systems, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed resolution; eliminates hidden cylinder wall scoring and turbo contamination

problems.recommendation: We recommend a full reconditioned engine if compression drops below 250 PSI across any cylinder or if coolant contamination has already damaged turbocharger bearings. Partial fixes on a heavily carbon-damaged OM651 rarely survive commercial delivery cycles, making a complete rebuilt unit the most cost-effective long-term investment for fleet operators.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM651 EGR & injector-related Mercedes Benz Vito engine failure

problems.h4: R9M503 Turbocharger Failure - Mercedes Benz Vito FWD Urban Routing Issue
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Vito 109 CDI FWD, 111 CDI FWD (W447, 2014-2019, R9M503)
problems.rootCause: The 1.6L Renault-sourced diesel suffers from turbo bearing starvation under heavy stop-start urban routing, compounded by EGR soot recirculation degrading oil quality and clogging feed lines.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Replacement & Oil Feed Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New turbo cartridge, revised oil supply/return pipes, full flush and DPF clean
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure; viable only if bottom end remains undamaged by oil loss

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt unit with reinforced turbo feed, supply & fit, ECU recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents recurring bearing failure; addresses hidden crank and conrod damage from oil starvation

problems.recommendation: For FWD Mercedes Benz Vito couriers, we only recommend a standalone turbo swap if oil pressure was never lost and diagnostic scans show zero bottom-end wear or metallic debris. If the engine experienced low-oil pressure warnings or knocking, a rebuilt R9M503 is the only reliable solution to prevent repeat failure under commercial payloads.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt R9M503 engine prices for Mercedes Benz Vito FWD

problems.h4: OM654 Low-Pressure EGR Cooler Cracking - Mercedes Benz Vito 2016+ Thermal Stress
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Vito 114 CDI, 116 CDI, 119 CDI (W447 facelift, 2016-present, OM654.920)
problems.rootCause: The low-pressure EGR system’s cooler undergoes extreme thermal cycling, developing hairline fractures that leak coolant into the intake, causing hydrolock risk or severe piston crown scoring.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cooler & VS30 Valve Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cracked cooler, install updated unit, replace VS30 valve, clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Stops coolant ingress; only effective if hydrolock has not occurred and compression is intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with updated EGR cooler, supply & fit, full diagnostic reset
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix; resolves cylinder wall scoring, bent rods, and coolant contamination

problems.recommendation: On a Mercedes Benz Vito W447, we advise replacing the EGR cooler immediately at the first sign of unexplained coolant loss or persistent white exhaust smoke. If the vehicle has cranked poorly, stalled, or hydrolocked, partial repairs will not survive commercial use, making a reconditioned OM654 the most dependable and financially sensible solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM654 Mercedes Benz Vito EGR cooler failure replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz Vito
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you are dealing with OM651 timing chain rattle, W639 chain elongation, or modern OM654 EGR thermal stress, ignoring Mercedes Benz Vito engine problems rapidly escalates repair costs and commercial downtime. EngineMarket connects you directly with vetted UK specialists who supply rebuilt and reconditioned engines fitted with upgraded components to eliminate known weak points from the factory design. Every unit includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide supply-and-fit delivery, ensuring your commercial van stays operational without the main dealer premium. Compare transparent pricing across our specialist network today and secure the right Mercedes Benz Vito engine solution for your exact variant.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz Vito Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz Vito and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz Vito Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every official displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz Vito is listed below, complete with compatible engine codes, production years and model-specific failure notes for accurate identification.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Vito Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vito 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre R9M503 unit was introduced exclusively for the front-wheel-drive W447 generation to maximise urban fuel economy and reduce emissions for courier and last-mile delivery operators. Sourced from Renault, this compact turbo-diesel prioritises low running costs and tighter turning circles over heavy towing capacity. It remains a high-volume choice for city-based fleet managers navigating daily stop-start routing.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging under sustained stop-start urban cycling
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vito 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vito 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Replacing the older 2.1-litre family in the facelifted W447, the OM654 2.0-litre diesel introduced stepped-bore technology and a refined low-pressure EGR system for strict Euro 6 compliance. This modern unit delivers improved thermal efficiency and smoother power delivery for commercial operators transitioning into cleaner air zones. It serves as the current-generation backbone for medium-duty commercial payloads.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vito 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vito 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1-litre OM651 became the definitive workhorse for late W639 and early W447 platforms, bridging the gap between legacy mechanical durability and modern common-rail injection efficiency. Widespread across 110 to 119 CDI variants, it offers a proven balance of low-end torque and highway cruising capability for long-distance tradespeople. Its market ubiquity makes it the most frequently quoted replacement unit across the UK.
commonFailurePoints: Rear-mounted timing chain tensioner seal leak (REC-005099)
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vito 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vito 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Powering the original W638 and pre-2010 W639 generations, the 2.2-litre OM611 and OM646 families established the Vito’s reputation for robust commercial longevity and straightforward mechanical servicing. These cast-iron block units prioritise low-end torque and high-mileage endurance, making them ideal for older fleet retention and heavy-load camper conversions. Their simple architecture continues to attract specialist rebuilders focusing on cost-effective longevity.
commonFailurePoints: cylinder head cracking near bore holes
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vito 2.2L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Vito Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vito 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre supercharged M271 petrol unit was introduced during the W639 lifecycle to cater to operators in low-emission urban zones or those requiring quieter running for passenger shuttle conversions. Unlike its diesel counterparts, this engine relies on an Eaton supercharger to deliver responsive throttle feel and smoother cabin refinement. It remains a niche but highly requested option for executive transport and specialist camper builds.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Vito 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vito 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Sitting at the top of the W639 range, the 3.5-litre M272 V6 petrol was engineered for premium passenger variants and high-spec Traveliner shuttles demanding effortless power delivery and reduced cabin vibration. This naturally aspirated unit trades heavy towing torque for refined highway performance, appealing to fleet managers prioritising passenger comfort. Its smooth operation continues to command premium replacement pricing from specialist rebuilders.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear wear causing internal rattling
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Vito 3.5L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz Vito Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major Mercedes Benz Vito engine changes across W638, W639 and W447 generations, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Mercedes Benz Vito launch established the mid-size van segment with robust mechanicals but early injector bore vulnerabilities.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation W638 chassis with rear-wheel drive layout
Introduction of OM611 diesel engine family across all commercial variants
Petrol engines added later in production cycle for passenger variants
Basic mechanical injection systems replaced by early common-rail technology
knownFor: Robust cast-iron block construction suitable for high-mileage commercial use
OM611 cylinder head cracking near injector bores on high-mileage units
Simple mechanical layout allows for straightforward specialist rebuilding
Early common-rail systems prone to injector seat erosion over time

keyChanges: Complete platform shift to W639 chassis with improved crash safety
OM646 diesel engine replaced OM611 with refined common-rail injection
Petrol engine range expanded to include supercharged M271 and V6 M272
Introduction of BlueTEC emissions technology on later models
knownFor: OM646 timing chain elongation requiring full replacement at 100k-140k miles
M271 supercharger clutch failure causing whining noise on petrol variants
Strong motorway fuel economy making these popular for long-distance trades
EGR valve sticking common on urban delivery vehicles with high idle time

Updated gearbox pairings for improved shift quality and efficiency

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz Vito Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes Benz Vito engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Mercedes Benz Vito engine replacement costs typically range from £1,100 to £4,500 for a rebuilt unit supply-only, depending on engine code, variant and condition. OM646 and OM611 units are generally cheaper, while modern OM654 and high-output OM651 engines command premium pricing due to complexity and demand.
keyPoints: OM651 rebuilt engines cost £1,500-£3,500 supply-only across most CDI variants
Older OM646 and OM611 units typically range £1,100-£2,900 for reconditioned supply
Modern OM654 2.0L diesel replacements start around £2,200 due to Euro 6 complexity
Supply and fit adds £800-£1,200 depending on workshop location and vehicle access
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Vito engine replacement prices from UK specialists now

items.question: What causes the Mercedes Benz Vito OM651 timing chain rattle on cold start?
items.answer: Mercedes Benz Vito OM651 timing chain rattle is caused by tensioner seal failure allowing oil pressure loss, leading to chain stretch and guide wear. This affects 2010-2024 W639 and W447 variants fitted with OM651.940 or OM651.955 engines, particularly between 70,000-120,000 miles.
keyPoints: REC-005099 recall addresses timing chain tensioner seal leaks on 2014 Mercedes Benz Vito models
Cold-start rattling indicates chain elongation requiring full engine-out replacement on W447 chassis
Ignoring the noise risks chain jump causing catastrophic valve and piston interference damage
Rebuilt OM651 units include updated tensioner seals and hardened guide rails as standard
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt OM651 Mercedes Benz Vito engines with updated timing components

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Mercedes Benz Vito?
items.answer: Replacing a Mercedes Benz Vito engine is worthwhile if the chassis is sound, mileage is under 150,000, and the van's market value exceeds £4,000. For a 2018 W447 with 80,000 miles, a £2,500-£4,000 rebuilt engine extends commercial life cost-effectively versus purchasing a replacement van.
keyPoints: W447 Vitos (2014+) retain strong residual values justifying engine investment over replacement purchase
Commercial downtime costs often make rapid supply-and-fit more economical than prolonged repairs
Rebuilt engines with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty protect against repeat failure expenses
Older W638/W639 models may not justify replacement unless chassis and bodywork are exceptional
items.cta: Find out if your Mercedes Benz Vito is worth an engine replacement with a free quote

items.question: Which Mercedes Benz Vito engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The OM646 2.2L diesel in pre-2010 Mercedes Benz Vito W639 models is widely regarded as the most reliable engine, offering robust cast-iron construction and simpler mechanical systems. However, all high-mileage units require proactive maintenance of timing chains and injectors to avoid costly failures.
keyPoints: OM646 engines in 111 CDI and 115 CDI variants demonstrate strong longevity with regular servicing
OM651 units are reliable if timing chain tensioner seals are replaced preventatively before 80,000 miles
Modern OM654 engines offer improved efficiency but require careful EGR system monitoring
Petrol M271 and M272 units are less common but generally robust if supercharger and balance shafts are maintained
items.cta: Compare reliable reconditioned Mercedes Benz Vito engines with specialist warranty coverage

items.question: How long does a Mercedes Benz Vito engine replacement take?
items.answer: A Mercedes Benz Vito engine replacement typically takes 2-4 working days for supply and fit when using a specialist network, depending on engine availability, vehicle access, and ECU coding requirements. W447 models may require additional time for AdBlue system recalibration and injector adaptation.
keyPoints: Supply-only engine delivery usually takes 24-72 hours from UK specialist stockists
Engine removal and refit requires 1-2 days for experienced Mercedes Benz Vito specialists
ECU coding and injector calibration add half a day for OM651 and OM654 equipped models
Mobile fitting services can reduce workshop booking delays for commercial fleet operators
items.cta: Find Mercedes Benz Vito engine supply and fit specialists with fast turnaround near you

items.question: Does a reconditioned Mercedes Benz Vito engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every reconditioned Mercedes Benz Vito engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering mechanical components, provided installation follows specialist guidelines. Warranty terms vary by supplier, so always confirm exact coverage, labour inclusion and claim procedures before purchase.
keyPoints: Standard warranty covers crank, pistons, bearings and cylinder head for 12 months unlimited mileage
Labour costs for warranty claims may require fitting at approved specialist workshops
Core return deposit is typically refundable upon receipt of the original failed engine unit
Extended warranty options up to 24 months are available on premium remanufactured units
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vito engines with confirmed 12-month unlimited mileage warranty

items.question: What is the difference between used and reconditioned Mercedes Benz Vito engines?
items.answer: A used Mercedes Benz Vito engine is a second-hand unit pulled from a donor vehicle with unknown internal condition, while a reconditioned engine has been stripped, inspected, and rebuilt with new wear components. Reconditioned units cost more but include warranty coverage and address known failure points like timing chains.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £800-£1,800 but carry no warranty beyond 30-90 days typically
Reconditioned engines (£1,500-£3,500) include new bearings, seals and updated tensioner components
Reconditioned OM651 units address REC-005099 timing chain tensioner recall vulnerabilities
Used engines require independent compression testing before purchase to avoid hidden damage
items.cta: Compare used versus reconditioned Mercedes Benz Vito engine prices and warranty terms

items.question: Can I fit a Mercedes Benz Vito engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible, fitting a Mercedes Benz Vito engine yourself is not recommended due to complex ECU coding, injector calibration and AdBlue system requirements on OM651 and OM654 engines. Professional supply and fit ensures warranty validity and avoids costly diagnostic errors that can disable the vehicle.
keyPoints: OM651 and OM654 engines require Mercedes-Benz Star Diagnosis tools for injector adaptation
Incorrect ECU coding after engine swap can trigger limp mode or prevent starting entirely
Heavy engine removal requires specialist lifting equipment and chassis support for safety
DIY installation typically voids supplier warranty on reconditioned or rebuilt engine units
items.cta: Find vetted Mercedes Benz Vito engine fitting specialists with proper diagnostic equipment

items.question: Are Mercedes Benz Vito engines compatible with Sprinter?
items.answer: Some Mercedes Benz Vito engines share codes with Sprinter models, but physical mounting points, ancillary brackets and ECU compatibility differ significantly between chassis generations. Never assume interchangeability - always verify exact engine code, variant and year match before purchasing a donor Sprinter engine for your Mercedes Benz Vito.
keyPoints: OM651 engines in Vito and Sprinter share internal architecture but differ in mounting and ancillaries
W447 Vito engines require specific HVAC clearance not present on Sprinter donor units
ECU calibration and immobiliser pairing prevent simple engine swaps between model lines
Specialist rebuilders can adapt Sprinter cores for Vito fitment but at additional cost
items.cta: Verify your Mercedes Benz Vito engine code before sourcing a compatible replacement unit

items.question: How do I find my Mercedes Benz Vito engine code?
items.answer: Your Mercedes Benz Vito engine code is stamped on the engine block near the injector pump or on a metal plate on the cylinder head. You can also find it on your V5C registration document under "engine number", or by entering your registration on the DVLA check tool for instant variant and code identification.
keyPoints: OM651, OM646 and OM654 codes are visible on the engine block timing cover area
V5C registration document lists engine number matching the factory-fitted unit code
DVLA online vehicle enquiry confirms variant and engine type using your number plate
Specialist suppliers can cross-reference chassis VIN to confirm exact engine compatibility
items.cta: Enter your Mercedes Benz Vito registration to instantly identify your engine code and get quotes
